era_context: Early Bronze Age in the Eastern Mediterranean and Near East. Egypt's Early Dynastic Period (c. 3100–2686 BCE) is in its early phase. Mesopotamia is in the Early Dynastic period. The Indus Valley Civilisation is developing. Writing systems are recent innovations.
disconfirming_evidence: No events identified for this year. The archaeological record for 3147 BCE is extremely sparse and undated to single-year precision.
historiographic_note: This year falls in the same poorly documented period as 3145–3146 BCE. No contemporary written records survive that can be confidently assigned to 3147 BCE. The historical record is reconstructed from archaeological evidence, radiocarbon dating, and later king lists, none of which permit year-by-year precision. The surviving record is heavily skewed toward elite and monumental contexts in the Near East and Egypt.
